question.text: Interest income received by a REIT from its SPV enjoys pass-through status. Under which section is it exempt at the REIT level?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Interest income received by a REIT from its SPV is exempt at the REIT level under Section 10(23FC). This is different from rental income which is exempt under Section 10(23FCA). The interest then becomes taxable in the hands of unit-holders. **The Trap:** Section 10(23FCA) is the most tempting distractor because it sounds almost identical. However, 23FCA is for rental income, while 23FC is for interest from SPV. Section 10(23FD) relates to dividend from SPV. Confusing these three sections is a common exam mistake. **Cross-Exam Context:** This level of section-specific detail is unique to NISM Series X-B; the exam tests ability to distinguish between closely related tax provisions. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Remember the mnemonic: 23FC = interest (FC for "Financial Charge"), 23FCA = rental (FCA, the "A" for "Asset rental"), 23FD = dividend (FD for "Fund Dividend").
